claims.claim_text: Luxembourg has a linguistic leave scheme of up to 200 hours for language courses.
claims.notes: Guichet states that linguistic leave exists and can be used for language learning under defined conditions.

claims.claim_text: The citizen's pact for intercultural living together includes introductory modules for people living or working in Luxembourg.
claims.notes: The Biergerpakt page says signatories can access information and training modules about intercultural living together and everyday life in Luxembourg.

claims.claim_text: Luxembourg already has public and private substitutes for language and integration support.
claims.notes: INLL offers public language learning and certification, Prolingua offers private language courses, and Guichet publishes civic integration modules.

claims.claim_text: Cross-border workers make up about 47% of Luxembourg's workforce.
claims.notes: A 2025 EU labour mobility report says Luxembourg’s cross-border worker share is 45.1%, so the card’s 47% figure is directionally right but not exact.
claims.next_action: re-search and verify with stricter source tier rules

claims.claim_text: No one sells a single relocation language + integration package.
claims.notes: The public substitutes already combine language learning, civic integration and work/life-in-country support, so the absolute uniqueness claim is too strong.
claims.next_action: falsify fully and rewrite or drop
